title: senecio paulsenii subsp. paulsenii
intro: Senecio paulsenii subsp. paulsenii, also known as Paul's Senecio or Jacobaea paulsenii, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its silymarin content, which is also found in milk thistle, making it a potential treatment for liver and gallbladder issues. The health benefits of Senecio paulsenii subsp. paulsenii include its ability to protect the liver from damage, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to promote liver health and overall well-being.
